summary: Tight December ’71 first set captured on a soundboard with superb mix despite some mild distortion, featuring standout renditions of Cumberland Blues and Broken‑Down Palace.
review: The recording offers a tight, high‑energy first set from the December 1971 run with an excellent soundboard mix that many reviewers call “suberb” (sic). While a few note slight muddiness and hiss on “Broken‑Down Palace,” the performance – especially Keith’s keyboards and the tighter feel without Mickey Hart – is praised as one of the best of ’71. The set is missing its second half, so listeners are encouraged to grab it elsewhere.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 5‑star ratings and highlight the show’s tight playing and superb mix; a few note minor distortion, leading to an overall strong combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mix is described as superb/clear; some listeners mention slight distortion, muddiness and hiss on the quiet “Broken‑Down Palace” section.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is exceptionally tight and energetic; second set is missing, so fans are advised to obtain it from the alternate source.

band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's keyboards receive praise for adding to the tight feel of the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Absence of Mickey Hart is noted positively, giving a "smaller ship, easier to turn" vibe.
